item.text: {"summary":"Proposed one new initial feature-search direction that avoids prior color, categorical, auxiliary-label, id, sky, and redshift-gate hypotheses by testing unsupervised manifold/proximity features from the competition feature distribution.","hypotheses":[{"title":"Unsupervised Photometric Locus Features","summary":"Represent each object by its unsupervised position, cluster affinity, and local density in color-magnitude-redshift space before applying simple balanced classifiers.","feature_family":"unsupervised_locus_density_embeddings","feature_strategy":"Build leak-free unsupervised features using only non-target columns from combined train and test: raw ugriz magnitudes, adjacent and broad color indices, redshift, and one-hot spectral_type/galaxy_population. Standardize numeric inputs, then add PCA components, KMeans or Gaussian-mixture cluster distances/probabilities, nearest-cluster margin features, and kNN local-density proxies such as mean distance to the 10/25/50 nearest neighbors. Do not use class labels, auxiliary labels, raw id, or target encodings; the hypothesis is whether object location on the empirical photometric manifold adds signal beyond hand-crafted colors alone.","baseline_model_panel":"Use a simple balanced panel: regularized multinomial logistic regression on scaled features, a balanced random-forest/extra-trees style model, and one class-weighted gradient-boosted tree model such as CatBoost with balanced class weights or LightGBM with balanced class weighting.","model_panel_rationale":"The linear model checks whether the embedding turns the problem into simple separable coordinates, while tree families test whether cluster distances and local-density thresholds expose nonlinear class regions; the panel is small enough to isolate feature value rather than tuning effects.","validation_strategy":"Use 5-fold StratifiedKFold on the competition train set, reporting balanced accuracy plus per-class recall. Fit unsupervised transformers on train+test features only inside the experiment because no labels are used, but keep all supervised model fitting strictly fold-local.","materialization_hint":"Create a staged preprocessing block that first derives standard photometric colors, scales the unsupervised input matrix, fits PCA plus a small range of cluster/density transformers once on train+test, appends their transformed features to train/test, and then evaluates the same fold splits across the simple model panel.","expected_signal":"If valuable, CV should improve most for QSO and boundary STAR cases, tree models should use cluster-distance or density features heavily, and fold scores should remain stable because the representation is label-free and low-dimensional.","risk":"Cluster features may duplicate existing color information or overfit synthetic train/test distribution quirks; kNN density can be runtime-heavy on 824k rows, so approximate neighbors or subsampling may be needed if exact distances are too slow.","sources":["https://arxiv.org/abs/1909.10963","https://arxiv.org/abs/astro-ph/0408504","https://arxiv.org/abs/1712.08955"]}]}
